summary: Frontend-only testing of 9 NEW dashboard + public-page changes for SEO Jalwa. Logged in with test@jalwa.com/test1234, traversed every new screen, modal, dialog, and toggle, and verified all advertised data-testids, copy strings, and behaviours. Everything passes after a one-time frontend restart that flushed a stale dev-server bundle (initial public-page checks failed; after `sudo supervisorctl restart frontend` they all pass).

issues: Hero subtitle still says 'Four intelligent modules that work together so you don't have to.' but the page now lists 5 modules (AI Visibility, AI Writer, Auto Publish, Social Autopilot, Analytics). Update copy to 'Five intelligent modules'.

Console accessibility warnings/errors: several Radix Dialog instances missing DialogTitle / aria-describedby (e.g., WordPress connect modal, Add Site dialog, Add Terms dialog). Wrap titles in VisuallyHidden or add descriptions.
Recharts ResponsiveContainer logs 'The width(-1) and height(-1) of chart should be greater than 0' on every render. Set explicit min-width/min-height on the chart container to silence the warning.

action_items: Update FeaturesPage hero subtitle from 'Four intelligent modules' → 'Five intelligent modules' to match the newly-added Analytics module.
Add DialogTitle (or VisuallyHidden wrapper) + aria-describedby to WordPressConnectModal, SiteSwitcher add-site dialog and PublishPage 'Add article topics' dialog to fix a11y console errors.
Give Recharts ResponsiveContainer on DashboardHome a defined parent height (e.g. h-12) to remove width(-1)/height(-1) warnings.

Note for future agents: the dev bundle did NOT pick up the latest HomePage.jsx/FeaturesPage.jsx edits until `sudo supervisorctl restart frontend` was issued — main agent may want to investigate why webpack hot reload silently missed those file changes (timestamps showed edits at 11:07 / 11:08 but bundle still served old strings until restart at 11:13).
critical_code_review_comments: SiteContext.jsx persists activeSiteId as JSON-stringified value ('"mystore"' instead of plain 'mystore'). Spec says localStorage `jalwa_active_site` should be the id. Currently it's JSON. Functionally fine (load() does JSON.parse) but doesn't match spec verbatim — confirm whether a string or JSON value was intended.

DashboardLayout currentPageLabel computation has redundant ternary `item.exact ? location.pathname === item.path : location.pathname === item.path` — both branches identical. Refactor to a simple equality + nested-route prefix match.
WordPressConnectModal hard-codes API_KEY 'jalwa_live_abc123xyz' at module scope — fine for mock, but mark clearly as mock so it isn't shipped to production.
Several files exceed component scope (PublishPage, ArticleSettingsPage). If they cross 700 lines consider splitting into sub-section components.
Multiple Radix Dialogs missing DialogTitle/Description — accessibility regression.
test_credentials: test@jalwa.com / test1234 (per /app/memory/test_credentials.md if maintained; supplied in review request)
seed_data_creation: Added a third site 'testsite.com' via Add-site dialog during testing — persisted in localStorage `jalwa_sites`. Main agent may want to clear localStorage before demoing.
context_for_next_testing_agent: All 9 changes (site switcher + localStorage, Auto Publish calendar/performance, Analytics page, Article Settings 10 sections + toggles, Team page, Connect Site rename + 3-step WordPress modal full flow ending in success screen, This-week strip on DashboardHome, sidebar 11-item ordering, public HomePage/FeaturesPage copy updates) all PASS. Only minor copy + a11y cleanup remains (see action_items). Important gotcha: webpack dev-server may serve stale bundle after edits — restart frontend if checks fail unexpectedly.
regression_check: Login (test@jalwa.com/test1234) → /dashboard works. Unauth /dashboard with cleared localStorage → redirects to /login (verified). Sidebar order matches spec exactly: ['Dashboard','Growth Score','AI Visibility','AI Writer','Auto Publish','Analytics','Social Autopilot','Article Settings','Connect Site','Team','Settings'] (11 items).
